commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e8066b79a9560b2e8de4e169f66929d96c94513c)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Windows doctor/update service repair to audit stale gateway service versions, restart running managed gateways after git updates, install running update repairs, and clean Startup-folder fallback launchers after Scheduled Task evidence.

PR surface: Source +99, Tests +278. Total +377 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction was established in this review; the linked issue has detailed Windows logs and the PR adds source-level regression tests for the path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft with compatibility-sensitive Windows startup changes needs the cleanup defect fixed and real proof accepted before merge.

target.title: fix(windows): repair doctor update fallback migration
target.body:
## Summary

- fixes Windows update-mode doctor repair so a stale managed gateway is activated only when the Windows service is already running; stopped services stay staged
- audits stale `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION` values and repairs version drift through the existing gateway service doctor path
- restarts the installed/running managed gateway after an interactive git update, while respecting externally managed service-repair policy
- removes legacy Windows Startup-folder fallback launchers (`.cmd` and hidden `.vbs`) only after Task Scheduler reports running evidence, including post-update restart migration

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87156.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts` - passed, 30 tests, including the hidden `.vbs` cleanup regression on amended `e8066b79a95`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts` - passed, 151 tests across daemon/cli/commands/auto-reply shards on amended `e8066b79a95`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-service-audit.test-helpers.ts src/daemon/service-audit.ts src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts` - passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-service-audit.test-helpers.ts src/daemon/service-audit.ts src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean after the hidden `.vbs` cleanup fix, no accepted/actionable findings, overall patch correct 0.82
- Fresh Windows probe: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26684876911 - passed on `blacksmith-16vcpu-windows-2025`; native Windows was Microsoft Windows NT 10.0.26100.0 / cmd 10.0.26100.4946, x64, PowerShell 7.4.11, Git 2.51.0.windows.1
- Fresh CI: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26684876898
  - `checks-windows-node-test`, `build-artifacts`, `check-lint`, `check-prod-types`, `check-test-types`, `check-guards`, and Node 22 compatibility passed
  - `checks-node-agentic-command-support` failed after `src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`, `src/daemon/service-audit.test.ts`, and `src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts` passed on both attempts; `test/vitest/vitest.daemon.config.ts` then hit the 300s no-output watchdog twice
  - `checks-node-agentic-commands-doctor` failed after `src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts` and `src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts` passed on both attempts; `test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts` then hit the 300s no-output watchdog twice
  - `checks-node-core-runtime-infra-state` failed after visible infra/state output including `src/infra/wsl.test.ts` passed on both attempts; `test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts` then hit the 300s no-output watchdog twice
- Testbox-through-Crabbox proof was blocked by missing Blacksmith auth (`blacksmith auth login` required). No additional direct AWS Crabbox lease was allocated because older AWS wrapper leases were already running.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Windows doctor/update migration now distinguishes running vs stopped managed gateways, detects stale service versions, restarts stale installed/running gateways after update, and only removes Startup-folder fallback launchers after Task Scheduler running evidence.

Real environment tested: native Windows runner through `windows-testbox-probe.yml` on `blacksmith-16vcpu-windows-2025`; local focused Vitest shards for daemon, CLI update, doctor command, and the CI auto-reply regression fixed by current `origin/main`; GitHub CI Windows/Linux selected lanes.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `ghx workflow run windows-testbox-probe.yml --repo openclaw/openclaw --ref main -f target_ref=fix/windows-doctor-startup-migration -f runner_label=blacksmith-16vcpu-windows-2025 -f keepalive_minutes=0 -f require_wsl2=false -f import_ubuntu_wsl2=true -f enable_wsl2_features=true`; focused local test/format/lint/autoreview commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: Windows probe run 26684876911 passed and confirmed native Windows execution; focused local tests passed 151 assertions; branch autoreview was clean after the hidden `.vbs` finding was fixed; WSL2 probe found `wsl.exe`, enabled VirtualMachinePlatform with exit 0, and reported `wsl_status_exit=0`, but distro list/import/exec still failed with exit -1 and `wsl2_ok=false`.

Observed result after fix: the patch is validated for native Windows and the service/doctor unit behavior; WSL2 execution remains blocked by the current runner environment rather than by this patch.

What was not tested: a successful WSL2 distro boot inside the Windows runner; a green broad Linux daemon/commands/infra CI sweep, because those broad shards reached the repo no-output watchdog after the touched tests had already passed.
target.author: vincentkoc
